behaviors.description: Command segmentation treats newline-separated statements as separate segments, not one monolithic block.
behaviors.justification: Failure boundary is parsing/segmentation logic itself (MOCK-MISS: no required module handoff). REAL-INFRA/LLM-DEP/MULTI-STEP are absent. Rejected Integration because behavior text is about segmentation semantics only, not cross-module state or persistence.
behaviors.test_description: Call the segmentation function with a multiline shell snippet (assignment lines + command line) and assert it returns distinct ordered segments rather than one combined segment.

behaviors.description: A multi-line shell block with variable assignments followed by `gh pr create` is recognized as a PR-create trigger.
behaviors.justification: Real failure appears at module boundary: segmented statements must be handed to trigger-classification logic correctly (MOCK-MISS yes). Unit of classifier alone could miss the segmentation-classification interaction that caused incident. Rejected System because behavior does not require real subprocess/network; input is command text.
behaviors.test_description: Run the hook’s parsing+classification path end-to-end in-process with a multiline block containing assignments then `gh pr create`; assert trigger classification is `pr_create` (not `not_a_trigger`).

behaviors.description: A similar multi-line block that ends with `gh issue create` is not incorrectly treated as a PR-create trigger.
behaviors.justification: Negative detection depends on same cross-module handoff (segmentation -> trigger matcher). A unit test of only one matcher function can miss boundary bugs in how final command segment is selected. Rejected System: no real OS/network/subprocess behavior is needed to expose this misclassification.
behaviors.test_description: Execute the in-process hook parsing+trigger-detection pipeline on multiline assignments + `gh issue create`; assert classification is not PR-create and no PR trigger event is emitted.

behaviors.description: When a PR-create trigger is detected, the hook can reconstruct and persist the PR identifier needed by the review loop state.
behaviors.justification: Behavior explicitly spans detection logic plus persistence contract (multiple modules + filesystem). Unit is too low because failure can occur in serialization/file-write boundary. Rejected System because local filesystem persistence is sufficient; no real external API/subprocess requirement in behavior text.
behaviors.test_description: Using a temp workspace, feed a detected PR-create event (including command/context needed for PR id reconstruction) through post-hook handler; assert a state file is written containing the expected PR identifier fields consumed by review loop.

behaviors.description: After PR-create detection, the review gate state is written in a form that the pre-tool enforcement hook can read and enforce.
behaviors.justification: This is a producer-consumer contract across two local modules/hooks via filesystem state (clear module interaction failure boundary). Unit tests on writer or reader alone can both pass while contract mismatch breaks enforcement. Rejected System because this can be caught with in-process hooks and temp filesystem; no real subprocess/network needed.
behaviors.test_description: In a temp directory, run post-hook path to write review-gate state after PR-create trigger, then invoke pre-tool enforcement hook against that state and assert enforcement behavior activates as expected.
